mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 550k signals rising demand for residential construction, boosting equity valuations through sector-specific momentum and positive macroeconomic spillovers.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 550k but 20-day forward returns are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 90 signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate profitability expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO exceeds 90 but 20-day forward returns are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and increases in CPIAUCSL signal accommodative monetary policy amid inflationary pressures, creating mixed valuation effects that produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive when FEDFUNDS < 1 and CPIAUCSL > 2, or interaction effect fails to materialize (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (rising FEDFUNDS) combined with slowing GDP growth (GDPC1) create headwinds for equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive during FEDFUNDS > 2 and GDPC1 < 0.5, or interaction effect fails (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 8% signals tightening labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive when UNRATE < 8, or Rank IC < 0.03 in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real sales (RSAFS) above 390 billion signals strong consumer demand, which drives equity valuations through sector-specific momentum and positive macroeconomic feedback loops.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when RSAFS exceeds 390, or Rank IC < 0.03 in triggered periods.
